title: Preventing and Recovering from Stroke in Indian Households
excerpt: India sees over 1.5 million stroke cases yearly, often from uncontrolled hypertension, diabetes, and high cholesterol. Learn how to prevent, detect, and recover from a stroke — with practical tips tailored for Indian lifestyles.
content:
Strokes, or "brain attacks," occur when blood flow to the brain is blocked or bursts, causing sudden weakness, speech slurring, or vision loss—India sees over 1.5 million cases yearly, often from uncontrolled hypertension, diabetes, and high cholesterol fueled by salty pickles, fried snacks, and sedentary jobs. Risk doubles post-55, but 80% are preventable with checks like annual HbA1c and lipid profiles.

Monitor BP daily at home (target <130/80 mmHg for diabetics) using affordable digital machines from pharmacies; reduce salt to 3-5g by swapping iodized namak for low-sodium versions and steaming veggies instead of tadka. Incorporate heart-protective foods like bajra khichdi, amla juice, and omega-3 from flaxseeds or affordable sardines—studies show turmeric's curcumin cuts clot risk by 25%. Walk 30 minutes post-dinner in colony parks to counter genetic predispositions common in North Indians.

Post-stroke, thrombolysis within 4.5 hours (clot-busters like tPA) restores flow—Dr. Pratik Kishore's neurovascular interventions at BLK save lives. Rehab includes speech therapy for swallowing rotis safely, occupational therapy with adaptive choppers for one-handed cooking, and yoga asanas like tadasana for balance. Family tip: Use the FAST test (Face droop, Arm weakness, Speech difficulty, Time to call ambulance); join BLK support groups for motivation.
author: Dr. Pratik Kishore

title: Daily Strategies to Control Epilepsy and Seizures
excerpt: Epilepsy impacts 10 million Indians. From medication adherence to pranayama, discover evidence-backed daily strategies — co-developed with neurologist Dr. Pratik Kishore — to reduce seizure frequency and live safely.
content:
Epilepsy impacts 10 million Indians, with seizures from abnormal brain electrical bursts—types include generalized tonic-clonic (full-body jerks, 2-3 minutes) or focal (one-sided twitching, odd tastes). Triggers like sleep deprivation from late office shifts, tobacco, or low blood sugar during Roza fasting affect 70% of cases.

Keep a seizure diary: log duration, witnesses, post-ictal confusion, and EEG-confirmed patterns—Dr. Pratik Kishore uses advanced video-EEG at BLK for precise AED dosing. Adhere 100% to meds; use reminders for twice-daily doses with breakfast dal or curd rice to avoid stomach upset.

Lifestyle wins: Pranayama (anulom vilom, 10 mins daily) lowers stress hormones. Safety: Helmets for two-wheelers, no solo swimming in monsoon pools; family action plan includes rescue breaths if >5 minutes. For refractory cases, BLK offers vagus nerve stimulation.
author: Dr. Pratik Kishore

title: Conquering Chronic Migraines with Remedies and Modern Care
excerpt: Migraines affect 15% of Indian women, triggered by hormonal cycles, summer dehydration, and everyday irritants like agarbatti smoke. Dr. Pratik Kishore shares a complete guide from home remedies to cutting-edge CGRP blockers.
content:
Migraines cause pulsating pain (often one-sided), nausea, and photophobia lasting 4-72 hours, affecting 15% of Indian women due to hormonal cycles, dehydration in Delhi summers, or triggers like agarbatti smoke and monosodium glutamate in pani puri. Auras (flashing lights, 20 minutes prior) distinguish them from tension headaches.

Diagnosis starts with a headache calendar tracking frequency (>15 days/month = chronic); MRI rules out secondary causes like sinusitis. Acute fixes: Sumatriptan tablets at onset, plus ginger chai for nausea; preventive propranolol for high-frequency attacks. Dr. Pratik Kishore tailors CGRP blockers like fremanezumab at BLK for pill-resistant cases.

Home arsenal: Hydrate with jeera water (2-3L/day), forehead cold compresses, and 7-8 hours sleep sans screens—avoid fasting skips by prepping oats upma. Acupuncture sessions help 60%. Botox every 12 weeks zaps triggers for festivals like Karva Chauth.
author: Dr. Pratik Kishore

title: Thriving with Parkinson's: Exercise, Meds, and Family Support
excerpt: 1-2% of Indians over 60 live with Parkinson's, often misdiagnosed as age-related shakiness. Dr. Pratik Kishore explains the gold-standard treatments, DBS surgery, and practical lifestyle strategies for patients and their families.
content:
Parkinson's disease (PD) stems from dopamine loss, causing rest tremors (pill-rolling), rigidity, bradykinesia, and postural instability—rising in India with 1-2% over-60 prevalence, misdiagnosed as "age-related shakiness." Non-motor signs like constipation from low-fiber diets or REM sleep issues appear early.

DaTscan confirms diagnosis; levodopa/carbidopa (Sindopa) is gold-standard, titrated slowly to avoid dyskinesia—take 30 mins before protein-heavy thalis. Dr. Pratik Kishore pioneers DBS at BLK, implanting electrodes to steady tremors in advanced PD, improving "on" time by 50%.

Amp movement with big-step walking (cue with metro beats), tai chi adapted as qigong in parks, or cueing with Bollywood songs for freezing gait. Diet: Separate proteins (post-levodopa) with carb-rich bajra roti mornings; mucuna pruriens (kaunch beej) as natural booster under guidance. Speech therapy for "festive voice" during Diwali; caregiver respite via BLK's multidisciplinary clinics keeps families strong.
author: Dr. Pratik Kishore

title: Empowering MS Patients: Symptoms, Nutrition, and Long-Term Management
excerpt: Multiple sclerosis is underdiagnosed in India, linked to low vitamin D and B12 deficiencies. Dr. Pratik Kishore walks through MRI-confirmed diagnosis, DMT therapy, and daily nutrition and exercise strategies for lasting quality of life.
content:
Multiple sclerosis (MS) demyelates nerves, leading to relapsing fatigue, optic neuritis (blurry vision), paresthesia (pins-and-needles), and spasticity—India reports 5-10 per 100,000, linked to low vitamin D from pollution-covered suns and vegetarian gaps in B12. Uhthoff's phenomenon worsens with summer heat.

MRI shows plaques; CSF analysis via lumbar puncture confirms. Start DMTs like ocrelizumab early—Dr. Pratik Kishore at BLK monitors via serial MRIs, halting progression in 70%. Symptom relief: Baclofen for leg stiffness, modafinil for fatigue.

Nutrition: 2000 IU vitamin D daily (test levels first), omega-3 from chia seeds in lassi, anti-inflammatory palak dal with turmeric. Exercise: Cool-water pool therapy pre-monsoon, balance board for ataxia. Cooling scarves beat heat; cognitive apps combat fog.
author: Dr. Pratik Kishore
